revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Iwa Shrine | image = [[File:Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Shrine grounds with sacred grove and main shrine | location = 539 Sōja, Matsumoto City, [[Nagano Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|14|18.15|N|137|59|27.51|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Iwa Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto)|Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|en|村社|12=draft}} | founded = Unknown | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|May 3|5月3日}} | Rituals = }} [[File:Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Iwa Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|伊和神社}}, ''Iwa-jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in {{ill|Matsumoto, Nagano|en|松本市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|長野県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Village Shrine|en|村社|12=draft}}. The official name is "Iwa-sha," but it is commonly referred to as "Iwa Shrine."[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm 松塩筑支部] (Nagano Prefecture Shrine Bureau). === Deities === * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto)[http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/iwa_title.htm 伊和神社] (Genbuko personal website). === History === In ancient times, {{ill|provincial governors|en|国司|12=draft}} visited all shrines in their provinces, starting from the {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}}. To facilitate this, a {{ill|Sōja|en|12=draft|12=draft}} was established near the provincial capital to enshrine the gods collectively for easier rituals. Iwa Shrine is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=draft|12=draft}}. The provincial capital was initially located near {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en}}, but it is believed to have moved to {{ill|Matsumoto, Nagano|en|12=draft|12=draft}} in the early Heian period."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. The area around the shrine has been called "Sōja" since ancient times, indicating the presence of a {{ill|Sōja|en}} and possibly the provincial capital.Shrine grounds information board. The name "Iwa" may derive from the nearby shrine "In'yaku-sha," which enshrined the official seal and key of the provincial storehouse. A theory suggests "In" (seal) transformed into "I," and "Yaku" (key) became "Wa" through scribal errors. Another theory posits that the shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, known as Iwa Myōjin, brought from the {{ill|Iwa Shrine (Hyōgo)|en|伊和神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Harima Province|en}}. The "Shinpū Tōki" (Edo period) mentions a local official, Satsuma-no-Kami, inviting Iwa Myōjin from Harima Province to create "Sōja Rokusha Daimyōjin," though this is considered a misinterpretation of earlier records."Sōja Village" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|en|村社|12=draft}} under the modern shrine classification system and listed as a {{ill|Kensaihei Shrine|en|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=draft}}.Shrine stone monument. == Sub-Shrines == * **Kokage Shrine** – Dedicated to silkworm cultivation, built during the height of the local silk industry. * **Sōja Iwa Daimyōjin** – Five shrines representing: ** {{ill|Usui Sadamitsu|en}} ** {{ill|Sakata no Kintoki|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|en}} ** {{ill|Watanabe no Tsuna|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Urabe no Suetake|en}} === Grounds === The shrine grounds feature numerous ancient {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|ケヤキ|12=draft}} trees. Two prominent zelkova trees at the entrance were formerly designated as a prefectural Natural Monument and are now protected as a city-level Natural Monument due to their age and declining health.[http://takara.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/city/158.html 伊和神社のケヤキ群] (Matsumoto City Cultural Property website). File:Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto) keyaki (left).JPG|Zelkova tree (left) File:Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto) keyaki (right).JPG|Zelkova tree (right) === Cultural Properties === ; Matsumoto City Designated Natural Monuments * **Iwa Shrine Zelkova Trees** – Designated November 11, 1975. == location == ; location * 539 Sōja, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine grounds information board * ''Nagano Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), Matsumoto City, Sōja Village entry == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shinano Ōmiya Shrine|en}} (Ueda City) – Another presumed Sōja of Shinano Province. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto)|12=draft}} * [http://takara.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/city/158.html Iwa Shrine Zelkova Trees] (Matsumoto City Cultural Property website) {{Sōja shrines}} [[ja:伊和神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65320}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[en:Iwa Shrine]][[es:Santuario Iwa]][[fr:Iwa-jinja]][[ja:伊和神社]][[simple:Iwa Shrine]][[zh:伊和神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|qid=Q812767|1=Shinto}}| name = Iwa Shrine | image = File:Iwa jinja 05.JPG | location = 539 Sōja, Matsumoto City, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.0875|N|134.58641667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}|Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|en|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | founded = Unknown | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|May 3|5月3日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Shiso|qid=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}} | architecture_style= {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} }} [[File:Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Iwa Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|伊和神社}}, ''Iwa-jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Matsumoto, Nagano|en|松本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Village Shrine|en|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}. The official name is "Iwa-sha," but it is commonly referred to as "Iwa Shrine."[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm 松塩筑支部] (Nagano Prefecture Shrine Bureau). === Deities === * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto)[http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/iwa_title.htm 伊和神社] (Genbuko personal website). === History === In ancient times, {{ill|provincial governors|en|国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial governors|qq=}} visited all shrines in their provinces, starting from the {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}}. To facilitate this, a {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} was established near the provincial capital to enshrine the gods collectively for easier rituals. Iwa Shrine is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. The provincial capital was initially located near {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, but it is believed to have moved to {{ill|Matsumoto, Nagano|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto, Nagano|qq=}} in the early Heian period."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. The area around the shrine has been called "Sōja" since ancient times, indicating the presence of a {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} and possibly the provincial capital.Shrine grounds information board. The name "Iwa" may derive from the nearby shrine "In'yaku-sha," which enshrined the official seal and key of the provincial storehouse. A theory suggests "In" (seal) transformed into "I," and "Yaku" (key) became "Wa" through scribal errors. Another theory posits that the shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, known as Iwa Myōjin, brought from the {{ill|Iwa Shrine (Hyōgo)|en|伊和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa Shrine (Hyōgo)|qq=}} in {{ill|Harima Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Province|qq=}}. The "Shinpū Tōki" (Edo period) mentions a local official, Satsuma-no-Kami, inviting Iwa Myōjin from Harima Province to create "Sōja Rokusha Daimyōjin," though this is considered a misinterpretation of earlier records."Sōja Village" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|en|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine classification system and listed as a {{ill|Kensaihei Shrine|en|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensaihei Shrine|qq=}}.Shrine stone monument. == Sub-Shrines == * **Kokage Shrine** – Dedicated to silkworm cultivation, built during the height of the local silk industry. * **Sōja Iwa Daimyōjin** – Five shrines representing: ** {{ill|Usui Sadamitsu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usui Sadamitsu|qq=}} ** {{ill|Sakata no Kintoki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata no Kintoki|qq=}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yorimitsu|qq=}} ** {{ill|Watanabe no Tsuna|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe no Tsuna|qq=}} ** {{ill|Urabe no Suetake|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe no Suetake|qq=}} === Grounds === The shrine grounds feature numerous ancient {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|ケヤキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zelkova serrata|qq=}} trees. Two prominent zelkova trees at the entrance were formerly designated as a prefectural Natural Monument and are now protected as a city-level Natural Monument due to their age and declining health.[http://takara.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/city/158.html 伊和神社のケヤキ群] (Matsumoto City Cultural Property website). File:Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto) keyaki (left).JPG|Zelkova tree (left) File:Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto) keyaki (right).JPG|Zelkova tree (right) === Cultural Properties === ; Matsumoto City Designated Natural Monuments * **Iwa Shrine Zelkova Trees** – Designated November 11, 1975. == location == ; location * 539 Sōja, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine grounds information board * ''Nagano Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), Matsumoto City, Sōja Village entry == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shinano Ōmiya Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiya Shrine|qq=}} (Ueda City) – Another presumed Sōja of Shinano Province. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto)|qq=draft}} * [http://takara.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/city/158.html Iwa Shrine Zelkova Trees] (Matsumoto City Cultural Property website) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|伊和神社|version=101175560|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:伊和神社]] {{Commons category|Iwa-jinja (Shiso)}} [[en:Iwa Shrine]] [[es:Santuario Iwa]] [[fr:Iwa-jinja]] [[simple:Iwa Shrine]] [[zh:伊和神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65320}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[en:Iwa Shrine]][[es:Santuario Iwa]][[fr:Iwa-jinja]][[ja:伊和神社]][[simple:Iwa Shrine]][[zh:伊和神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|qid=Q812767|1=Shinto}}| name = Iwa Shrine | image = File:Iwa jinja 05.JPG | location = 539 Sōja, Matsumoto City, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.0875|N|134.58641667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}|Status = (Presumed) {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|en|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | founded = Unknown | Main_Hall = | Other_names = | festivals = {{nihongo|May 3|5月3日}} | Rituals = | prefecture= {{ill|Shiso|qid=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}} | architecture_style= {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} }} [[File:Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Iwa Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|伊和神社}}, ''Iwa-jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Matsumoto, Nagano|en|松本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. Its modern status is a former {{ill|Village Shrine|en|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}. The official name is "Iwa-sha," but it is commonly referred to as "Iwa Shrine."[http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/syouenchiku.htm 松塩筑支部] (Nagano Prefecture Shrine Bureau). === Deities === * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto)[http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/iwa_title.htm 伊和神社] (Genbuko personal website). === History === In ancient times, {{ill|provincial governors|en|国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial governors|qq=}} visited all shrines in their provinces, starting from the {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}}. To facilitate this, a {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} was established near the provincial capital to enshrine the gods collectively for easier rituals. Iwa Shrine is presumed to have served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}. The provincial capital was initially located near {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda, Nagano|qq=}}, but it is believed to have moved to {{ill|Matsumoto, Nagano|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto, Nagano|qq=}} in the early Heian period."Shinano Province" in {{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|editor=平凡社}}. The area around the shrine has been called "Sōja" since ancient times, indicating the presence of a {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} and possibly the provincial capital.Shrine grounds information board. The name "Iwa" may derive from the nearby shrine "In'yaku-sha," which enshrined the official seal and key of the provincial storehouse. A theory suggests "In" (seal) transformed into "I," and "Yaku" (key) became "Wa" through scribal errors. Another theory posits that the shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, known as Iwa Myōjin, brought from the {{ill|Iwa Shrine (Hyōgo)|en|伊和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa Shrine (Hyōgo)|qq=}} in {{ill|Harima Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Province|qq=}}. The "Shinpū Tōki" (Edo period) mentions a local official, Satsuma-no-Kami, inviting Iwa Myōjin from Harima Province to create "Sōja Rokusha Daimyōjin," though this is considered a misinterpretation of earlier records."Sōja Village" in ''Nagano Prefecture Place names''. In the {{ill|Meiji period|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|en|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine classification system and listed as a {{ill|Kensaihei Shrine|en|神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensaihei Shrine|qq=}}.Shrine stone monument. == Sub-Shrines == * **Kokage Shrine** – Dedicated to silkworm cultivation, built during the height of the local silk industry. * **Sōja Iwa Daimyōjin** – Five shrines representing: ** {{ill|Usui Sadamitsu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usui Sadamitsu|qq=}} ** {{ill|Sakata no Kintoki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata no Kintoki|qq=}} ** {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yorimitsu|qq=}} ** {{ill|Watanabe no Tsuna|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watanabe no Tsuna|qq=}} ** {{ill|Urabe no Suetake|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe no Suetake|qq=}} === Grounds === The shrine grounds feature numerous ancient {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|ケヤキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zelkova serrata|qq=}} trees. Two prominent zelkova trees at the entrance were formerly designated as a prefectural Natural Monument and are now protected as a city-level Natural Monument due to their age and declining health.[http://takara.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/city/158.html 伊和神社のケヤキ群] (Matsumoto City Cultural Property website). File:Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto) keyaki (left).JPG|Zelkova tree (left) File:Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto) keyaki (right).JPG|Zelkova tree (right) === Cultural Properties === ; Matsumoto City Designated Natural Monuments * **Iwa Shrine Zelkova Trees** – Designated November 11, 1975. == location == ; location * 539 Sōja, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine grounds information board * ''Nagano Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), Matsumoto City, Sōja Village entry == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shinano Ōmiya Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiya Shrine|qq=}} (Ueda City) – Another presumed Sōja of Shinano Province. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Iwa-jinja (Matsumoto)|qq=draft}} * [http://takara.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/city/158.html Iwa Shrine Zelkova Trees] (Matsumoto City Cultural Property website) {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|伊和神社|version=101175560|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:伊和神社]] {{Commons category|Iwa-jinja (Shiso)}} [[en:Iwa Shrine]] [[es:Santuario Iwa]] [[fr:Iwa-jinja]] [[simple:Iwa Shrine]] [[zh:伊和神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65320}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shiso, Hyogo]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Matsumoto, Nagano]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shisō]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
